messages.content: Where were you when the lights went out?
I-I was by the records desk, I think. When it got dark I just froze because of my Achluophobia, okay? I know that sounds stupid, but I couldn't make my legs move.
Why didn't you help secure the room?
I wanted to, I really did, but Achluophobia is not me being dramatic. When the whole place went black, my chest locked up and I just stood there trying not to panic.
Did you have a flashlight with you?
Y-yeah, but that doesn't mean anything. It's just a high-quality flashlight, that's all, the kind that stays bright and, uh, works really well under pressure.
People said your flashlight felt warm.
W-warm? I mean, maybe a little? It's just being high quality, like I said. Good gear gets used a lot for regular work, that's not suspicious by itself.
What were you doing before the blackout?
Sorting the transfer sheets and trying not to mess up the numbering again. Then everything went dark and my Achluophobia kicked in, and after that it's all kind of a shaky blur.
Did you move toward the stone?
N-no, no. That's the point, I froze. If the room is dark, I stop thinking straight, and I definitely don't start marching toward giant ancient objects.
Why are you so defensive about the flashlight?
Because everyone keeps looking at it like it's proof of something. It's just a high-quality flashlight, okay? I bought it because cheap ones flicker and that makes the dark worse for me.
What does Achluophobia do to you exactly?
It makes everything clamp down at once. My hands shake, my throat closes up, and if the lights die suddenly I just freeze like my brain stopped sending orders.
Then how do you explain still having your gear ready?
I-I always keep my gear organized. That's not the same as being brave. The flashlight was there because I carry it all the time, and it's just high quality, not some weird special thing.
Did you hear anything in the dark?
A couple people yelling, something heavy maybe, and my own heartbeat being way too loud. Once Achluophobia hits, details get messy because I'm mostly trying not to freak out.
Why not tell us about your fear earlier?
Because it's embarrassing, that's why. People hear Achluophobia and start treating you like a joke, and I already mess up enough without handing them extra reasons.
You know your flashlight sounds important, right?
I know how it sounds, yeah, and that's why I'm nervous. But it really is just a high-quality flashlight. I keep saying that because it's true, not because I'm hiding some huge secret.
Could you have left the room during the blackout?
I d-don't think so. When the dark hits me like that, I get stuck. Achluophobia doesn't exactly make me bold and athletic.
What were your hands doing during all this?
Shaking. One was on the edge of the desk and the other was probably around my flashlight strap, just because I grab at whatever feels familiar when I panic.
What do you want us to understand?
That being scared can look guilty when it's not. I froze because of Achluophobia, and the flashlight is just high quality gear I rely on too much when I get nervous.
